channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Why NRL rules allowed for Eels' three-point winner
item.description: The NRL says an unusual referee call, which allowed the Parramatta Eels to attempt a penalty goal after scoring in golden-point, was the correct decision.

media:group.media:description: Mitch Moses was as confused as anyone with Parramatta being given a penalty kick after a field goal.

item.title: Maroons dealt massive blow ahead of State of Origin series
item.description: Queensland selectors have been forced to cope with the news one of their best playmakers will miss up to eight weeks with his ankle injury.

media:group.media:description: Tom Dearden will be sidelined for up to eight weeks.

item.title: Your guide to the weekend: Eternity, Wuthering Heights and new Meg Mac
item.description: What we're watching, reading and cooking this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jo Joyce, Al Newstead, Hannah Story, and Velvet Winter

media:group.media:description: Lena Dunham dishes the gossip in Famesick.

item.title: Live: Winless Dragons booed by fans at half-time after conceding six tries
item.description: St George Illawarra fans have booed the Dragons off the field after the Red V conceded six tries in the first half against the Newcastle Knights. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Kade Reed of the Dragons looks down as Sam Walker of the Roosters converts the try of Mark Nawaqanitawase during the NRL Round 8 match between the St. George Illawarra Dragons and Sydney Roosters at the Sydney Football Stadium in Sydney, Saturday, April 25, 2026.

item.title: CSIRO to persist with job cuts despite $387 million funding injection
item.description: Australia's national science agency CSIRO will persist with job cuts to up to 350 roles despite an extra $387.4 million in funding announced from the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Gore

media:group.media:description: The federal government has announced it will invest an additional $387.4 million into the CSIRO over the next four years.

item.title: Massive ruby found in Myanmar, where gem mining has links to junta
item.description: Miners have unearthed a large ruby in Myanmar, which produces up to 90 per cent of the world's rubies.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: A rare large high-quality ruby has been found in conflict-torn Myanmar.

item.title: US importing more beef as demand soars and prices rise
item.description: The United States is importing more beef from Australia as its own supplies dwindle and demand soars.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brann

media:group.media:description: Americans are forking out record money to buy beef.

item.title: Live: Kangaroos take on Swans in Saturday opener ahead of Cats-Pies clash
item.description: The Sydney Swans travel to Docklands to face North Melbourne in the first of four AFL games on Saturday, before tonight's big clash between Geelong and Collingwood at the MCG. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton, Chris De Silva, and Andrew McGarry

media:group.media:description: STRALIA - MAY 09: Hayden McLean of the Swans and Wil Dawson of the Kangaroos contest the ruck during the round nine AFL match between North Melbourne Kangaroos and Sydney Swans at Marvel Stadium, on May 09, 2026, in Melbourne, Australia. (Photo by Daniel Pockett/Getty Images)

item.title: Iron Maiden's documentary is a victory lap celebrating 50 years
item.description: The Iron Maiden documentary is a 50-year history of heavy metal's flashiest band as told by their biggest fans.
item.dc:creator: Dan Condon

media:group.media:description: Iron Maiden: Burning Ambition features plenty of footage from throughout the band's 50-year career.

item.title: Concerns for welfare of Victorian man last seen near Bibbulmun track
item.description: Samuel Whitsed, 27, was last seen on February 18 after he was dropped off near the popular hiking trail in Perth's south-eastern fringe, and police continue searching for him.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Rendall

media:group.media:description: Police continue their search for Victorian hiker Samuel Whitsed, months after he was last seen.

item.title: Extreme fatigue illness linked with changes to immune cells: study
item.description: Ella Engel saw many specialists before she was diagnosed with myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome. Her blood may help researchers understand the condition.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Roberts

media:group.media:description: Ella Engel, pictured with dad John, was hospitalised multiple times as her condition deteriorated.

item.title: Kremlin tells Australian embassy in Kyiv to evacuate amid parade tensions
item.description: The Australian embassy in Ukraine is on high alert after the Kremlin warned staff to evacuate ahead of possible "massive missile strikes" this weekend, as Moscow prepares to hold a scaled-down military parade.
item.dc:creator: Syan Vallance

media:group.media:description: Vladimir Putin will attend Saturday's military parade in Moscow, amid a temporary ceasefire.

item.title: Do you call it a willy-willy, whirly wind or dust devil?
item.description: It's not unusual to see willy-willies kicking up dust across WA, but did you know the name of the weather phenomenon comes from WA's north-west?
item.dc:creator: Kimberley Putland

media:group.media:description: A willy-willy near Whim Creek, Pilbara, Western Australia.

item.title: 'Powerful' symbol faces removal from the heart of Chinese community
item.description: The 26-year-old artwork is facing removal after the City of Sydney identified termite damage and cracking in the timber.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Lin Li's Golden Water Mouth sculpture has been in Chinatown since 1999.

item.title: Zoo owner's free land offer ignored as promised vet centre yet to be built
item.description: In 2022, the Victorian government promised to build a new wildlife veterinary clinic in south-west Victoria. Despite being offered access to free land, it is yet to build the facility.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Wilkes

media:group.media:description: Mark Treweek says he offered the government land to build the veterinary clinic on for free.

item.title: Palestine Marathon returns after two-year hiatus
item.description: Thousands of local and international athletes competed in the 10th International Palestine Marathon in Bethlehem, in the West Bank, after two years of war halted the event.

media:group.media:description: Runners can dream of freedom in the West Bank city of Bethlehem.

item.title: 'This is pretty not normal': Lucas Herbert on verge of maiden LIV victory
item.description: Lucas Herbert was good in Thursday's opening round, but was even better on Friday's second to extend his lead at the LIV Golf Virginia as he hunts a maiden career win on the league.

media:group.media:description: Lucas Herbert was flawless in round two.

item.title: 'Where's the pastizzis?' Secrets from a lifetime of Maltese cheese-crafting
item.description: Eighty-year-old cheesemaker Philippa Abela has been practising a craft passed down by her mother, who emigrated with her from Malta when Philippa was a child.
item.dc:creator: Liam McNally

media:group.media:description: Pastizzi, traditional pastries from Malta, are one of Philippa's specialties.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise passengers to be chartered home from Spain
item.description: The MV Hondius is expected to arrive in Tenerife on Sunday at midday local time, with passengers then to be chartered back home if their countries agree to send planes.

media:group.media:description: Eight people have been confirmed to have contracted hantavirus aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship. Three have died.

item.title: Stomach aches, vermin, aerated water: Trademarks tell us how we used to live
item.description: The patent and trademark records held in the national archives show anxiety and ambitions in everyday life in the early days of colonial Western Australia.
item.dc:creator: Emma Wynne

media:group.media:description: Pills and potions for sale at Boans Ltd pharmacy, Perth, 1932.

item.title: Świątek sheepish after training accident injures coach
item.description: Francisco Roig will think twice about betting against his new charge Iga Świątek after the veteran coach tore his Achilles tendon in a volley session in Rome.

media:group.media:description: Iga Świątek did not immediately realise how bad her coach's injury was.

item.title: Spying controversy hits richest prize in world football
item.description: Southampton has been accused of spying on rivals Middlesbrough ahead of their crucial Championship play-off clash at the Riverside on Saturday.

media:group.media:description: Southampton will get to grips with Middlesbrough in the play-off semifinal this weekend.

item.title: Much-loved tramway Clydesdales hit with mystery flu-like illness
item.description: The majestic horses that pull the historic tramway at Victor Harbor on the South Australian coastline are under the weather. Locals hope they will soon return.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Horn

media:group.media:description: Archie is resting while he shakes off his illness.

item.title: Client abuse and excessive workloads impacting vets
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry makes more than 30 recommendations to address increase of poor veterinary mental health outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kelly

media:group.media:description: Sophie Putland had a lifelong love for all animals, but especially horses.

item.title: 'My heart nearly stopped': Rare new bug spotted three times since discovery
item.description: A new type of bug, celebrated for the "really cool" way it assassinates prey, has been named in honour of traditional custodians in the northern Australia region where it was discovered.

media:group.media:description: The Larrakia feather-legged assassin was recently discovered among leaf litter in the Northern Territory.
